anchor.snippet: the Langlands program is a set of conjectures about connections between number theory
Objects for which Langlands conjectures can be stated
The Artin reciprocity law applies to a Galois extension of an algebraic number field whose Galois group is abelian
For non-abelian Galois groups and higher-dimensional representations of them, L -functions can be defined in a natural way
Langlands then generalized these to automorphic cuspidal representations
conjectured that every Artin L -function arising from a finite-dimensional representation of the Galois group of a number field is equal to one arising from an automorphic cuspidal representation
Over local fields this is expected to give a parameterization of L -packets of admissible irreducible representations of a reductive group over the local field
The functoriality conjecture states that a suitable homomorphism of L -groups is expected to give a correspondence between automorphic forms
Langlands constructs the Langlands dual group
One of his conjectures states that these L -functions satisfy a certain functional equation generalizing those of other known L -functions
He then goes on to formulate a very general "Functoriality Principle"
The geometric Langlands program, suggested by Gérard Laumon following ideas of Vladimir Drinfeld
In 2024, a 9-person collaborative project led by Dennis Gaitsgory announced a proof of the (categorical, unramified) geometric Langlands conjecture
The Langlands correspondence for GL(1, K ) follows from (and is essentially equivalent to) class field theory

annotations.label: Langlands conjectures over archimedean local fields

anchor.snippet: Langlands proved the Langlands conjectures for groups over the archimedean local fields
Lusztig's classification of the irreducible representations of groups of Lie type over finite fields

annotations.label: Wiles' modularity theorem as Langlands instance

anchor.snippet: Andrew Wiles ' proof of modularity of semistable elliptic curves over rationals can be viewed as an instance of the Langlands reciprocity conjecture
In 1998, Laurent Lafforgue proved Lafforgue's theorem verifying the global Langlands correspondence for the general linear group
In 2018, Vincent Lafforgue established one half of the global Langlands correspondence
Philip Kutzko
Gérard Laumon , Michael Rapoport , and Ulrich Stuhler proved the local Langlands correspondence for the general linear group
Michael Harris and Richard Taylor proved the local Langlands conjectures for the general linear group
Laurent Fargues and Peter Scholze recast the local Langlands correspondence in geometric terms using the Fargues–Fontaine curve
prove a version of the geometric Satake equivalence for the Fargues–Fontaine curve
Their work also formulates a conjectural categorical form of local Langlands
Ngô Bảo Châu proved the " fundamental lemma "
